author: Electricks94
body: **PR description:** This PR introduces a `SoAMultiView`. The `SoAMultiView` holds an `std::array` of `ConstViews` and keeps track of the valid range of each view. It can be seen as the equivalent to the [MultiSpan](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/blob/master/DataFormats/Common/interface/MultiSpan.h) for SoAs. The range of each view can be user-defined; by default, the `view.metadata().size()` is taken. The `SoAMultiView` is useful if `PortableCollections` templated on the same SoA-Layout from multiple producers forms a single iteration space in downstream algorithms. Due to the design of using a fixed-sized array to hold the `ConstViews` it is possible to pass an `SoAMultiView` to a kernel by value like a `ConstView` is usually passed to a kernel. This makes the `SoAMultiView` a drop-in replacement for kernels that use a single `ConstView`. Also, this PR uses the `SoAMultiView` in the CA instead of [SiPixelRecHitExtendedAlpaka](https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/blob/master/RecoLocalTracker/SiPixelRecHits/plugins/alpaka/SiPixelRecHitExtendedAlpaka.cc) that copies the data of two `PortableCollections` to a single `PortableCollection`. With the `SoAMultiView` the copy routine is fully replaced. ## Timing Measurements run on 2x AMD EPYC 9534 with 4x L40 GPUs. Configuration: 16 jobs with 16 threads/16 streams each, sample of 1000 TTbar 200PU events. Two configurations have been used for benchmarking: 1. HLT:75e33_timing (Referred to as timing later on): ```python cmsDriver.py step2 -s L1P2GT,HLT:75e33_timing \ --process HLTX \ --conditions auto:phase2_realistic_T35 \ --geometry ExtendedRun4D110 \ --era Phase2C17I13M9 \ --customise SLHCUpgradeSimulations/Configuration/aging.customise_aging_1000 \ --eventcontent FEVTDEBUGHLT \ --filein $ALL_FILES \ --mc \ --inputCommands="keep *, drop *_hlt*_*_HLT, drop triggerTriggerFilterObjectWithRefs_l1t*_*_HLT" \ -n 1000 \ --no_exec \ --output={} \ --python_filename timing_config.py ``` 2. HLT:75e33_trackingOnly (Referred to as trackingOnly later on): ```python cmsDriver.py Phase2 -s L1P2GT,HLT:75e33_trackingOnly \ --processName=HLTX \ --conditions auto:phase2_realistic_T35 \ --geometry ExtendedRun4D110 \ --era Phase2C17I13M9 \ --customise SLHCUpgradeSimulations/Configuration/aging.customise_aging_1000 \ --eventcontent FEVTDEBUGHLT \ --filein=$ALL_FILES \ --mc \ --inputCommands="keep *, drop *_hlt*_*_HLT, drop triggerTriggerFilterObjectWithRefs_l1t*_*_HLT" \ -n 1000 \ --procModifiers ngtScouting \ --no_exec \ --output={} \ --python_filename timing_config.py ``` | Baseline timing; throughput: 52.2 0.1 ev/s | SoAMultiView timing; throughput: 52.2 0.1 ev/s | |-------------|-------------| | | | | Baseline trackingOnly; throughput: 312.6 3.5 | SoAMultiView trackingOnly; throughput: 316.8 3.9 ev/s | | | | The data of the timing plots can be found [here](https://maholzer.web.cern.ch/circles/web/piechart.php?colours=default&data_name=data&groups=hlt&show_labels=true&show_animations=true&threshold=0) ## Memory usage GPU memory usage have been measured while running the timing measurements using the output from `nvidia-smi`, thus the setup is exactly the same. **PR validation:** This PR has been tested locally running `runTheMatrix.py -l limited -i all --ibeos`. The physics comparison of using the `SoAMultiView` with the baseline can be found [here](https://maholzer.web.cern.ch/SoAMultiViewComperisonWithBase/) FYI: @felicepantaleo
title: SoAMultiView
